rulings.Case Title: JAMES BARAGAR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, September 24, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Gibbs & Cox, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present evidence refuting that defendant¿s role with regard to the USS Rogers was: 1) anything more than the provision of architectural, engineering and procurement services, 2) defendant did not manufacture, sell or supply any product for that vessel and 3) while it may have specified in its drawings that asbestos-containing products be included in that vessel, defendant did not participate in whole or in part in the design of any asbestos-containing products in that vessel. Accordingly, there is no basis for either a strict liability or negligence claim against defendant. (Del Mar Beach Club Owners Assn. v. Imperial Contracting Co. (1981) 123 Cal. App. 3d 898, 914 (strict liability does not lie against engineer and architect); Allied Properties v. John A. Blume & Associates (1972) 25 Cal. App. 3d 848, 855-56 (strict liability does not lie against a marine engineer); Weseloh Family Limited Partnership v. K.L. Wessel Construction Co. (2004) 125 Cal. App. 4th 152, 164-73 (based on extensive discussion of and application of Biankanja and Bily, no duty under negligence law owed by engineer to the owner or general contractor where the engineer provided design services pursuant to a contract with a subcontractor but had no contract with either the building owner or general contractor)). Plaintiffs¿ objections to the declarations of Mr. Sollecito and Admiral Horne as to the character and nature of the work provided by defendant to the Navy (i.e., architectural and engineering services, not manufacture, sale or supply of products) are overruled. (To be continued.)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES BARAGAR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Gibbs & Cox, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, September 24, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Gibbs & Cox, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment. Continued from previous page: Defendant¿s objections to exhibits D, E, F, G, H, paragraph 9 of Dr. Richard Cohen¿s declaration, paragraph 4 of Mr. Fening¿s declaration, and the portions of Mr. Kenneth Cohen¿s declaration for this case that relate to the character and nature of the work provided by defendant to the Navy are sustained. The court has considered the declaration of Mr. Dyer and, even assuming that defendant objects to that declaration, the objection is overruled. The tentative ruling does not rest on nor does the court address the validity of the government contractor defense. = (220/HEK)
